zh.lifeSummary: 當Rhys ap Llywelyn於大約1230年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾特雷萊-阿-特福斯時，他的父親Llywelyn Ap MADOG18歲，母親Isabella de Braose18歲。他於1272年與Catrim Gruffudd在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾蒙姆斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rhys ap Llywelyn于大约1230年出生在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾特雷萊-阿-特福斯时，他的父亲Llywelyn Ap MADOG18岁，母亲Isabella de Braose18岁。他于1272年与Catrim Gruffudd在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾蒙姆斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Rhys ap Llywelyn was born about 1230, in Trelech-ar-bettws, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Llywelyn Ap MADOG, was 18 and his mother, Isabella de Braose, was 18. He married Catrim Gruffudd in 1272, in Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

ko.lifeSummary: Rhys ap Llywelyn(이)가 대략 1230년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르 트렐렉h-아-베트w스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Llywelyn Ap MADOG(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella de Braose(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1272년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 몬모우스에서 Catrim Gruffudd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rhys ap Llywelyn родился около 1230 г. в местности трелеч-ар-беттвс, кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Llywelyn Ap MADOG было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Braose, 18.
